data.title: Foreign media reports: Iran's willingness to abandon nuclear weapons in exchange for peace is beginning to emerge, causing crude oil prices to fall.
data.description: According to Sky News Arab, Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister stated, "Iran is prepared to abandon its nuclear program if the United States can provide a satisfactory alternative." Yesterday, reports surfaced that Iran had been secretly contacting the United States to reach an agreement and end the conflict, but Iran subsequently denied these reports as false. In short, these are clear signs of de-escalation, and investors may only need to wait for the United States or Israel to announce that their goals have been achieved to see the market rejoice. Following the Deputy Foreign Minister's comments, the market reacted positively, with stocks jumping while the dollar and oil prices fell. (Jinshi)

data.title: Primark Appoints CEO Amid Potential Spinoff
data.description: Primark has announced the appointment of its new CEO as the company considers a potential spinoff. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move as Primark navigates its future business plans. Meanwhile, Senior has received another takeover proposal, indicating ongoing interest in the company. As the day unfolds, industry insiders are also discussing the top Oscar films to watch, providing a cultural touchpoint amid business developments.

data.title: Azerbaijan Defense Ministry Responds to Iranian Attack on Nakhchivan
data.description: Azerbaijan's Defense Ministry has issued a statement regarding a recent attack by Iran on the Nakhchivan Autonomous Republic. According to Jin10, the ministry emphasized that such actions will not be ignored and highlighted the importance of maintaining regional stability. The incident has raised concerns about escalating tensions between the two countries, with Azerbaijan asserting its right to defend its territorial integrity. The ministry's statement underscores the potential for increased military readiness in response to perceived threats. The situation remains tense as both nations navigate the complexities of regional geopolitics.

data.title: Italy Ready to Intervene Amid Economic Impact of Iran Crisis
data.description: Italy's Foreign Minister has announced that the government is prepared to take action to mitigate the economic repercussions of the ongoing crisis in Iran. According to Jin10, the minister emphasized the importance of addressing the situation promptly to safeguard Italy's economic interests. The crisis in Iran has raised concerns globally, with potential implications for international markets and trade. Italy's proactive stance highlights the government's commitment to maintaining economic stability amid geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Japan Engages with NATO to Utilize Defense Technology Accelerator
data.description: Japan is reportedly in discussions with NATO to leverage its defense technology accelerator, according to Jin10. This collaboration aims to enhance technological advancements in defense systems, reflecting Japan's strategic interest in strengthening its security capabilities. The move is part of broader efforts to foster international cooperation in defense technology development, aligning with NATO's objectives to integrate innovative solutions into its defense strategies. The discussions underscore the importance of technological innovation in maintaining global security and defense readiness.

data.title: Swiss Franc Rises Amid Middle East Tensions and Inflation Data
data.description: The Swiss franc has strengthened due to renewed safe-haven demand triggered by Middle East conflicts. According to Jin10, higher-than-expected Swiss inflation data on Wednesday also supported the franc, reducing the likelihood of the Swiss National Bank lowering interest rates into negative territory. However, Michael Pfister from Commerzbank noted in a report that the Swiss National Bank has expressed increased willingness to intervene in the foreign exchange market due to the Middle East tensions, potentially limiting the franc's gains. Active intervention may be the next step.

data.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Denies Explosion in Kaveh Industrial City
data.description: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard's Saveh County division has issued a statement refuting reports of an explosion in Kaveh Industrial City. According to Jin10, Iranian media sources had initially reported the incident, but the Revolutionary Guard has clarified that no such event occurred. The statement aims to dispel rumors and provide accurate information regarding the situation in the industrial area. The denial comes amid heightened tensions and concerns over security in the region.

data.title: Citrea Hosts First Offline Meetup in Seoul to Discuss Bitcoin Rollup Project
data.description: Citrea, a Bitcoin zero-knowledge rollup project, is organizing its first offline meetup in Seoul on March 5. According to NS3.AI, the event will include a presentation by the co-founder and CTO, Esad Yusuf Atik, who will discuss mainnet updates and the project's roadmap. The project launched its mainnet in January and subsequently established its foundation.

data.title: ECB Vice President Hints at Policy Shift Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: European Central Bank Vice President Luis de Guindos has indicated that the ECB may alter its policy stance if inflation expectations change due to the situation in Iran. According to Jin10, de Guindos emphasized the importance of monitoring geopolitical developments and their potential impact on inflation. The ECB is closely observing the situation to ensure that its monetary policy remains aligned with economic conditions. The ongoing tensions in Iran could lead to shifts in inflation expectations, prompting the ECB to reassess its approach to maintaining price stability. The central bank remains committed to its mandate of ensuring stable prices and supporting economic growth in the eurozone.

data.title: Russia Criticizes U.S. and Israel for Lack of Restraint in Iran Conflict
data.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has expressed concern over the ongoing conflict in Iran, criticizing the United States and Israel for not showing signs of restraint. According to Jin10, the ministry highlighted the absence of efforts from both nations to halt the bloodshed within Iran's borders. The statement underscores the geopolitical tensions surrounding the region, as international observers continue to monitor the situation closely. The Russian government has called for a more cautious approach to prevent further escalation and instability in the area.

data.title: South Africa Faces Inflation Concerns Amid Rising Oil Prices Due to Middle East Conflict
data.description: South Africa's Finance Minister, Enoch Godongwana, has expressed concerns over the potential inflationary impact of rising oil prices caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. According to Jin10, Godongwana highlighted that the government's established buffer mechanisms are expected to help maintain the country's debt consolidation plans on track. This week, Brent crude oil prices surged nearly 16% as shipping through the critical Hormuz Strait, a passage for one-fifth of the world's oil transport, came to a halt. In an interview, Godongwana noted that South Africa is a 'price taker' when it comes to oil imports, adding that the situation poses inflationary risks for the nation. 'This war is concerning,' he stated.

data.title: BSB listed on Bitget PoolX, unlocking 1.13 million BSB from locked BTC.
data.description: Bitget PoolX is launching the BSB project, with a total airdrop of 1,133,000 BSB. There are two BTC staking pools. In the regular pool, users can lock up to 30 BTC to unlock 400,000 BSB. In the dynamic cap pool, the unlock limit is tiered based on the user's trading volume over the past 15 days, with a maximum lock-up of 35 BTC per user, unlocking 733,000 BSB. The staking channel is open from 16:00 on March 5th to 16:00 on March 10th (UTC+8). Users with a positive net BTC deposit during the event will receive a 5% BTC interest rate bonus coupon after PoolX ends. Additionally, first-time PoolX participants who meet the net deposit requirements will receive a 10% BTC interest rate bonus coupon. Net top-up period ends on March 9th at 12:00 (UTC+8). For more details, please visit the Bitget official platform.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister condemns US attack on Iranian warship
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi said today (March 5) that the United States, by launching a surprise attack on an Iranian warship in international waters, "will ultimately regret the precedent it set." Araqchi stated on social media that the Iranian frigate, carrying approximately 130 people, was a "guest of the Indian Navy" and was attacked in international waters 2,000 miles (approximately 3,218 kilometers) off the Iranian coast without any prior warning. US Defense Secretary Hergsays said on March 4 that a US submarine torpedoed and sank an Iranian warship "believing itself to be safe in international waters" in the Indian Ocean on March 3. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iraqi Kurdistan Pledges Peace Amidst Regional Tensions
data.description:
On March 5, Nechirvan Barzani, the President of the Kurdistan Region in northern Iraq, issued a statement emphasizing the region's commitment to peace and its refusal to engage in conflicts or military actions that endanger civilian lives. According to Jin10, Barzani called for unity among all political forces, parties, and communities within the Kurdistan Region to collectively uphold national responsibilities.

Aziz Ahmed, Deputy Chief of Staff of the Kurdistan Regional Government, also addressed the situation on social media platform X, refuting claims that Iraqi Kurds had crossed borders, labeling such reports as "clearly false." This statement comes in response to earlier reports from the United States suggesting that Iraqi Kurds had launched a ground offensive within Iran.

data.title: Sungrow Partners with Egyptian Contractor for Major Solar Project
data.description: Sungrow has announced a significant collaboration with Egyptian project contractor Hassan Allam to supply centralized photovoltaic inverter solutions for the Benban Solar Park in Aswan, Egypt. According to Jin10, this agreement marks another major solar project for Sungrow in North Africa, following their supply to the Abydos energy storage station in Egypt.

data.title: ECB Vice President De Guindos: Iranian Attack Heightens Uncertainty
data.description: European Central Bank Vice President Luis de Guindos has expressed concerns over the increased uncertainty following recent attacks by Iran. According to Jin10, De Guindos highlighted the geopolitical tensions that could impact economic stability and market conditions. The situation has raised questions about potential repercussions for the global economy, particularly in terms of energy prices and investor sentiment. De Guindos emphasized the need for vigilance and careful monitoring of developments as the situation unfolds.

data.title: Iran Open to Abandoning Nuclear Program if U.S. Offers Satisfactory Alternative
data.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister has announced that Iran is willing to abandon its nuclear program, provided that the United States proposes a satisfactory alternative. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid ongoing tensions between the two nations over nuclear capabilities and international security concerns. The Iranian official emphasized the need for a viable and acceptable solution from the U.S. to address the nuclear issue. This development could potentially pave the way for renewed diplomatic negotiations and efforts to resolve the longstanding conflict over Iran's nuclear ambitions.

data.title: South Korea Bans Travel to Iran Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: South Korea has announced a travel ban to Iran starting Thursday at 6 PM, according to Jin10. This decision by the South Korean Ministry of Foreign Affairs comes amid escalating tensions in the region. The move aims to ensure the safety of South Korean citizens in light of the current geopolitical climate. Travelers are advised to adhere to the new regulations and avoid non-essential travel to the area. The ministry will continue to monitor the situation closely and provide updates as necessary.
